channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Dell stock skyrockets 32% for its best day ever as AI server revenue soars
item.description: Dell reported its fastest pace of revenue growth since returning to the public market in 2018, with AI server revenue soaring 757% over last year.

item.title: Trump ends Iran meeting without announcing ‘final determination’ on deal
item.description: Trump's Truth Social statement left unclear which of his conditions are already part of a deal that negotiators are working on to pause the U.S.-Iran war.

item.title: Hegseth praises Asian allies for 'burden-sharing,' calls out China's role in the region
item.description: Hegseth said that China cannot impose its hegemony on U.S. partners and allies in the region.

item.title: Stocks close at record highs with tech leading the way again. Nasdaq gains 8% in May
item.description: U.S. equities closed at record highs on Friday, while crude prices slipped, helping the major averages score a winning month, boosted by technology.

item.title: Software stocks wrap up best month since 2001 as talk of 'SaaSpocalypse' subsides
item.description: Snowflake and Okta both saw record stock pops this week as investors found favor in their AI software strategies.

item.title: Wall Street rides high into June on hopes that the war will soon come to an end. Here's what's ahead
item.description: The stock market continues to bet on some sort of resolution being found with Iran. But there is also no shortage of other risks heading into a new month.

item.title: SpaceX skeptics have added reason for concern after Musk comments diverge from IPO filing
item.description: In a post on X, Elon Musk offered details about SpaceX's deal with Anthropic that weren't included in the company's IPO prospectus.

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks Trump's $1.8 billion 'weaponization' fund
item.description: A U.S. judge on Friday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump's administration from setting up a nearly $1.8 billion fund to compensate victims of what Trump has called government "weaponization."

item.title: Brent oil price posts biggest monthly loss in six years as market counts on a U.S.-Iran deal
item.description: Oil prices fell Friday after President Trump said he would meet in the White House Situation Room to make a final decision about a deal with Iran.

item.title: Mercedes-Benz may be shut out of U.S. market under bill aimed at Chinese automaker ownership
item.description: Mercedes-Benz's largest individual shareholder is BAIC, a Chinese state-owned automaker. Sources told CNBC that exemptions in the legislation would not apply.

item.title: Student loan borrowers will have two new repayment options come July 1. Here's how to pick one
item.description: Starting July 1, millions of federal student loan holders will have two new ways to repay their debt. Here's what to know about the additional options.

item.title: Roth IRA owners may need a second retirement account to claim the new Saver's Match
item.description: The Saver's Match program, which starts in 2027, is for lower- and moderate-income retirement savers — but Roth IRA owners may face a hurdle to get the money.

item.title: CNBC's Official Global Soccer Team Valuations 2026: Here's how the top 30 clubs in the world stack up
item.description: The world's 30 most valuable soccer teams are worth an average of $2.66 billion, according to CNBC's official team valuations.

item.title: Jim Cramer says Dell’s blowout quarter sets up a crucial week for AI stocks
item.description: CNBC’s Jim Cramer said Dell Technologies’ blockbuster quarter reignited enthusiasm around AI and data center stocks.

item.title: What the Blue Origin rocket explosion means for Amazon's satellite ambitions
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: Trump's name must be removed from Kennedy Center, judge rules
item.description: "The Kennedy Center is an institution that belongs to the American people, not to Donald Trump," said Rep. Joyce Beatty, who sued over the renaming.

item.title: Morgan Stanley says these 'dividend hopefuls' are poised to outperform if they initiate a payout
item.description: Dividend stocks may help boost investor returns over time, and Morgan Stanley thinks a number of companies are in a position to begin paying them.

item.title: Iran war cost: Average U.S. household paying $450 more on gas and energy
item.description: Higher energy costs can force consumers to raid their savings and lean more on debt to cover expenses.

item.title: Postal Service proposes requiring states to provide mail-in ballot voter lists
item.description: USPS issued the proposal one day after a judge declined to block Trump’s mail-voting order, allowing implementation efforts to continue.

item.title: IRS unveils new health savings account limits for 2027
item.description: The IRS has increased the health savings account limits for 2027. Here's what you need to know.

item.title: Tokens or humans? The new corporate trade-off
item.description: AI is costing far more than companies expected, forcing CFOs into a new trade-off between tokens and humans and posing a risk the market hasn't priced in.

item.title: Delta Business Traveler won't replace a premium business travel card, but it's free
item.description: Delta's free Business Traveler program includes Calm, LinkedIn and coworking discounts.

item.title: Blue Origin warns of rocket explosion debris after failed New Glenn test at Cape Canaveral
item.description: Jeff Bezos' rocket maker suffered a setback on Thursday as its New Glenn rocket went up in flames.

item.title: Trump DOJ 'lawfare' fund temporarily blocked by judge as suit proceeds
item.description: The DOJ has faced strong criticism for the creation of a $1.8 Anti-Weaponization Fund which could compensate allies of President Donald Trump.

item.title: Disney's ABC files early broadcast licenses renewal. FCC Chair vows to 'follow the facts'
item.description: Disney said it filed renewals for eight broadcast station licenses "under protest," after the FCC launched an early review years ahead of schedule.

item.title: Bondi defends handling of Epstein files to House panel
item.description: President Donald Trump fired Pam Bondi in April after being displeased with her handling of DOJ files about sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Nvidia is investing billions into this emerging technology that could change the AI industry
item.description: Photonics is considered to be a more efficient alternative to the current process of transferring data using electricity, which could be crucial to the AI boom.

item.title: As SpaceX IPO nears, traders think it's a near-certainty Musk becomes the first trillionaire
item.description: With buzz about a record-shattering IPO for SpaceX, traders are confident Elon Musk will advance to trillionaire status before 2027.

item.title: The ins and outs of short squeezes — and how to avoid getting burned by them
item.description: Staying in stocks over the long haul to compound wealth is the best move.

item.title: Costco falls after earnings. But shareholders could get a special boost soon
item.description: Some on Wall Street believe the leading warehouse club could soon issue a special dividend.
